content.problemStatement:
Chef is given a sequence of integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.

Chef considers a contiguous subsequence Al,Al+1,…,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_rAl​,Al+1​,…,Ar​ (where 1≤l≤r≤N1 \le l \le r \le N1≤l≤r≤N) fruitful if it satisfies the condition
∣Al−Ar∣=max(Al,Al+1,…,Ar)−min(Al,Al+1,…,Ar) .|A_l - A_r| = \mathrm{max}(A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r) - \mathrm{min}(A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r) \,.∣Al​−Ar​∣=max(Al​,Al+1​,…,Ar​)−min(Al​,Al+1​,…,Ar​).

Please help Chef find the number of fruitful contiguous subsequences of the sequence AAA.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of fruitful contiguous subsequences.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: All contiguous subsequences, i.e. [1][1][1], [2][2][2], [3][3][3], [1,2][1,2][1,2], [2,3][2,3][2,3] and [1,2,3][1,2,3][1,2,3], are fruitful.
